items.briefing:
A vulnerability in Apple's "Hide My Email" feature has been discovered that can expose users' real email addresses, so Apple device users should be cautious until a fix is confirmed. Separately, research shows Australian banks are falling behind on DMARC email protection, meaning phishing emails impersonating banks are more likely to reach customers — something all staff should watch for carefully.

Multiple reports highlight that AI tools are increasingly being used across Australian workplaces without proper oversight, creating security risks organisations may not even be aware of. Be mindful of what information you enter into AI tools — avoid sharing client details, student records, or internal documents.

featured.title: Australian banks lag on strict DMARC email protection
featured.quote: Australian banks' weak email security leaves customers vulnerable to preventable phishing attacks.
